name: Argentinian Stew in a Pumpkin Shell
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Beef stew meat, cut into 1 1/2-in. cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim any excess fat from the beef and cook with onion and garlic in oil until meat is browned.
2. Add tomatoes, green pepper, 1 tablespoon salt, 1/2 teaspoon pepper, sugar, apricots, white potatoes, sweet potatoes, and broth to the pot. Cover and simmer for 1 hour.
3. Cut the top off the pumpkin and discard it. Scoop out the seeds and stringy membrane.
4. Brush the inside of the pumpkin with melted butter and sprinkle lightly with salt and pepper.
5. Stir in sherry and corn into the stew, then spoon into the pumpkin shell.
6. Place the shell in a shallow pan and bake at 325°F for 1 hour, or until the pumpkin meat is tender.
7. Place the pumpkin in a large bowl and ladle out stew, scooping some of the pumpkin with each serving.
